properties.remark: SPOTTER REPORTS POOR VISIBILITY FROM BLOWING DUST.
0.75 MILE VISIBILITY FROM BLOWING DUST.
VERY POOR VISIBILITY, DUE TO BLOWING DUST.
SPOTTER SAW THE FUNNEL CLOUD PICKING UP DEBRIS FROM THE GROUND IN AN OPEN FIELD. THE ESTIMATED WIDTH OF THE TORNADO WAS APPROXIMATELY 25 YARDS. THIS OCCURRED IN A RURAL LOCATION - NO DAMAGE.
SPOTTER SAW TORNADO TOUCH DOWN AND WENT OVER DIRT ROAD. TORNADO WENT BACK UP INTO CLOUD QUICKLY. OCCURRED IN A RURAL LOCATION WITH NO DAMAGE.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS HAIL REPORT FROM 1 SE SPRING CREEK. SIGNIFICANT HAIL TO AROUND HALF DOLLAR SIZE. HAIL ACCUMULATED TO AROUND 1 IN DEPTH. HAIL COVERING ROADS.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS HAIL REPORT FROM 3 SSE SPRING CREEK. NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED HAIL AROUND DIME SIZE, WITH SOME STONES THE SIZE OF QUARTERS. TIME CORRECTED.
HALF INCH TO 1 SIZE HAIL. HAIL COVERED THE GROUND TO AT LEAST 2-3 INCHES DEEP. SIGNIFICANT HAIL ACCUMULATION ON THE ROAD.
NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED HAIL AROUND DIME SIZE, WITH SOME STONES THE SIZE OF QUARTERS.
FLASH FLOODING COVERED PARKING LOTS IN THE AREA. POSSIBLE DAMAGE TO CARS IN THE FLOOD WATERS.
FLOODING VIDEOS AND PHOTOS OF LAST CHANCE ROAD. POSSIBLE MINOR ROAD DAMAGE.
MESONET STATION, ALRN2, ANTELOPE LAKE RAWS
SEVERE WIND GUSTS FROM A LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS.
A VIDEO WAS SHARED SHOWING A TORNADO, WHICH APPEARS TO HAVE A DUST WHIRL ON THE GROUND. TORNADO WAS AT AN ESTIMATED ELEVATION AROUND 6500-7000FT. LOCATION WAS ESTIMATED BASED ON REPORT AND RADAR.
PUBLIC REPORT VIA SOCIAL MEDIA OF WATER OVER INTERSTATE 80 EAST OF CARLIN TUNNEL. HAIL HAD ALSO ACCUMULATED ON ROADWAY.
NVDOT TRAFFIC WEBCAMS SHOWED WATER RUNNING OVER INTERSTATE 80 NEAR EMIGRANT PASS. HAIL ALSO ACCUMULATED A FEW INCHES IN SPOTS. TIME ESTIMATED BASED OFF RADAR AND WEBCAM IMAGE TIME.
SHERIFF REPORTS WATER ON ROADWAY NEAR CARLIN TUNNEL.
EUREKA COUNTY SHERIFF REPORTS WATER ON STATE ROUTE 278 NEAR MILE MARKER 70.
COAL MINE CANYON. LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS MOVED ACROSS THE AREA.
18 OF FLOWING WATER ACROSS US 95 IN SE PORTIONS OF TONOPAH.
PUBLIC REPORTS VIA SOCIAL MEDIA VIDEOS AND PICTURES OF STREET FLOODING IN TONOPAH.
SCAFFOLDING COLLAPSED FROM GUSTY OUTFLOW WINDS BETWEEN 50 TO 55 MPH. NO INJURIES BUT THE ROADWAY IS BLOCKED. AIRPORT ALSO GUSTED TO 51 MPH. NWS OFFICE GUSTED TO 43 KNOTS.
DEBRIS FLOW FROM BROAD FIRE REPORTED OVER FREMONT ROUTE AND APPROACHING MANLY LANE IN HADLEY / CARVERS AREA OF BIG SMOKEY VALLEY.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS DEBRIS FLOW REPORT FROM 2 NNE DUCKWATER. HEAVY RAIN OVER DUCKWATER CAUSED A DEBRIS FLOW OVER STATE ROUTE 379. THE DEBRIS FLOW WAS ESTIMATED TO BE 5 -7 FEET WIDE AND 2 INCHES DEEP.
HEAVY RAIN OVER DUCKWATER CAUSED A DEBRIS FLOW OVER STATE ROUTE 375. THE DEBRIS FLOW WAS ESTIMATED TO BE 5 -7 FEET WIDE AND 2 INCHES DEEP.
TIMBER MOUNTAIN RAWS GUSTED TO 58 MPH WITH THUNDERSTORM OUTFLOWS.
COMBS CANYON RAWS.
BRIEF LANDSPOUT CONFIRMED FROM THE CALLAGHAN PEAK WEBCAM.
NDOT REPORTED WATER ON THE ROAD FOR US 93 AT MM 70 AND STATE ROUTE 229 AT RUBY VALLEY.

NDOT CALLED AGAIN TO REPORT TWO AREAS WITH WATER/ROCKS/MUD OVER THE ROAD. THE FIRST AROUND 4 MILES WEST OF THE SR 229 AND US 93 JUNCTION AND THE OTHER AREA 4 MILES NORTH ON US 93 FROM THE SAME JUNCTION. CREWS WERE NEEDED TO REMOVE THE DEBRIS OFF THE ROAD. THIS IS MOST LIKELY FROM THE ECHO BURN SCAR.
BLOWING ASH/DUST OFF THE MARTIN BURN SCAR REPORTED BY TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER. VISIBILITY AROUND 3 MILES OR LESS, LIKELY TO DOWN TO ONE MILE TO THE EAST OF THE SPOTTER.
